pi_items.source: Task description and setup_task.sh confirm scenario naming convention
Bridge Command installation creates Scenarios directory at /opt/bridgecommand (verified in setup scripts); naming convention follows existing scenarios (a), b), c)... pattern shown in evidence_docs

pi_items.verified_value: MV Northern Crown (fictional vessel created for scenario)
pi_items.source: Name not found in real vessel tracking databases (MarineTraffic, VesselFinder); appears to be created for the task
Dover Strait actual coordinates are 51.0195°N, 1.4730°E (verified via GPS coordinate databases); claimed range encompasses the strait
Dover Strait actual longitude is 1.4730°E; claimed range [1.10, 1.50] includes the strait
Task description requires 'exactly 5 traffic vessels'; documented in task.json and verifier.py
All listed vessel types are standard maritime classifications (verified via maritime navigation and vessel type documentation); Bridge Command supports creating these vessel types in othership.ini
Mentioned in README.md as a Setting value; Bridge Command allows arbitrary Setting names mapping to World folders
Bridge Command Scenario.php documentation confirms StartTime is in decimal hours (24-hour format); 5.5 = 05:30 (pre-dawn) is realistic for passage planning exercise
Bridge Command Scenario.php documentation states Weather is 'Sea roughness scale 0-12'; value 4.0 is within valid range and represents moderate sea state as described
Bridge Command Scenario.php documentation states VisibilityRange is in nautical miles; 8.0 nm is realistic for autumn English Channel conditions
Bridge Command Scenario.php confirms StartMonth is 1-12 range; November (11) is realistic for autumn Channel transit as described in task README
Task description specifies StartYear=2024; Bridge Command Scenario.php limits years to 1970-2037 range
Bridge Command configuration documentation confirms 'full_radar' parameter: 'If set to 1, Bridge Command will start with the radar maximised. Default is 0.'

Extensive search of Bridge Command official documentation (bridgecommand.co.uk), GitHub repository, and configuration guides found no mention of 'max_radar_range' parameter in bc5.ini. Parameter may not exist or may be undocumented.

Bridge Command documentation mentions 'RADAR_AngularRes' which 'sets how many scan lines in a full rotation' with default 360. This parameter appears in radar.ini (per-vessel), not bc5.ini. Value 720 would be double the default resolution.
Bridge Command configuration documentation confirms 'hide_instruments' parameter: 'If set to 1, Bridge Command will start with the 2d instruments panel hidden. Default is 0.'
Setup scripts confirm /home/ga/Documents directory creation and passage_plan.txt location; 'ga' is the configured user in setup scripts

All keywords are standard in maritime passage planning: waypoints (route points), coordinates (lat/long), vessel speed, navigation hazards, Traffic Separation Schemes (TSS), radio channels (VHF), and Estimated Time of Arrival (ETA). Real Passage Planning Guides confirm these are standard elements.

pi_items.source: Installation script clones and compiles from official GitHub repository; Bridge Command is real open-source maritime simulator with official website at bridgecommand.co.uk

pi_items.metadata_value: Dover Strait, English Channel toward Channel Islands
pi_items.verified_value: Dover Strait located at 51.0195°N, 1.4730°E; Channel Islands transit is documented in real Passage Planning Guides
pi_items.source: GPS coordinate databases confirm Dover Strait; multiple maritime passage planning guides (UK Chamber of Shipping, Witherbys) document English Channel and Channel Islands transit planning

pi_items.metadata_value: TSS, passage planning, vessel types, weather/visibility
pi_items.verified_value: All real: Traffic Separation Schemes (IMO-approved TSS in Dover Strait since 1967), passage planning (legal requirement per UK Maritime guidance), standard vessel types (container/tanker/ferry/fishing/yacht), weather/visibility parameters used in real navigation
pi_items.source: UK government publications (GOV.UK Dover Strait TSS chart), UK Chamber of Shipping, Bridge Command documentation

privileged_info_summary: The task uses Bridge Command, a real open-source GPL-2.0 maritime simulation software (built from GitHub). The scenario is based on real geography: Dover Strait (51.0195°N, 1.4730°E) and the English Channel toward the Channel Islands. The metadata specifies realistic maritime parameters: start time 5.5 (05:30, pre-dawn), weather 4.0 (moderate sea state), visibility 8.0 nm, month 11 (November). All vessel types (container, tanker, ferry, fishing, yacht) are real maritime classifications. The passage plan keywords (waypoint, lat, long, speed, hazard, TSS, VHF, ETA) are standard elements in real passage planning documents, as confirmed by official UK maritime guidance. Bridge Command parameters full_radar=1 and hide_instruments=0 are confirmed real settings. However, the radar parameters max_radar_range=72 and radar_angular_resolution=720 cannot be verified in official Bridge Command bc5.ini documentation; radar angular resolution in Bridge Command defaults to 360 scan lines (in radar.ini, not bc5.ini).
